content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 22, 1991

A. Kay Vinson
220 E. Mt. View
Barstow, CA 92311

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-041

Dear Ms. Vinson:

You have requested advice concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

FACTS

As Municipal Records Administrator for the City of Barstow,
you are designated in the city's conflict of interest code
to file the Form 730 Statement of Economic Interests. Your
husband works for Lockheed Support Systems Inc. which does not own
real property, have an office, or sell products or services in
Barstow. The corporation does purchase nominal supplies in
Barstow.

QUESTION

Are you required to disclose your community property interest
in the income received by your spouse from Lockheed Support
Systems Inc.?

CONCLUSION

You must report your community property interest in your
spouse's income if the source of the income is of the type
required to be disclosed by your disclosure category and if the
source of income is located in or does business in your

1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

A. Kay Vinson
page 2


jurisdiction.  Because Lockheed Support Systems Inc. purchases
supplies in your jurisdiction, it is doing business in your
jurisdiction for purposes of the Act.

ANALYSIS

     Section 82030 defines "income" of an official to include "a
payment received ... including any community property interest in
the income of a spouse."

     A source of income is located in or does business in your
jurisdiction if it is or any parent, subsidiary or otherwise related
entity has an interest in real property in the jurisdiction, or
does business or plans to do business in the jurisdiction, or has
done business within the jurisdiction during the two years prior
to the time any statement is required.  (Section 82030(a).)

     Section 82035 defines "jurisdiction" as "... the state with
respect to a state agency and, with respect to a local government
agency, the region, county, city, district or other geographical
area in which it has jurisdiction...."

     If an entity has business contacts within a jurisdiction,
there is a potential for conflict of interests and therefore
justification for requiring disclosure of the official's interest
in that entity.  (In re Baty (1979) 5 FPPC Ops 10, copy enclosed.)
An entity which makes regular or substantial purchases or business contacts in a
jurisdiction has sufficient business contacts to constitute "doing
business" in the jurisdiction.  Therefore, based upon the
information provided in your request for advice, Lockheed's
purchases of supplies in your jurisdiction means that Lockheed is
doing business in your jurisdiction.  You are thus required to
disclose your community property interest in the income received
by your spouse from Lockheed.

     If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          Scott Hallabrin
                                          Acting General Counsel

                                          [signature]

                                          by: Colleen McGee
                                              Political Reform Consultant
                                              Technical Assistance and
                                              Analysis Division

jurisdiction. Because Lockheed Support Systems Inc. purchases supplies in your jurisdiction, it is doing business in your jurisdiction for purposes of the Act.

sections.analysis:
Section 82030 defines "income" of an official to include "a payment received ... including any community property interest in the income of a spouse."

A source of income is located in or does business in your jurisdiction if it or any parent, subsidiary or otherwise related entity has an interest in real property in the jurisdiction, or does business or plans to do business in the jurisdiction, or has done business within the jurisdiction during the two years prior to the time any statement is required. (Section 82030(a).)

Section 82035 defines "jurisdiction" as "... the state with respect to a state agency and, with respect to a local government agency, the region, county, city, district or other geographical area in which it has jurisdiction...."

If an entity has business contacts within a jurisdiction, there is a potential for conflict of interests and therefore justification for requiring disclosure of the official's interest in that entity. (In re Baty (1979) 5 FPPC Ops 10, copy enclosed.) An entity which makes regular or substantial purchases in a jurisdiction has sufficient business contacts to constitute "doing business" in the jurisdiction. Therefore, based upon the information provided in your request for advice, Lockheed's purchases of supplies in your jurisdiction mean that Lockheed is doing business in your jurisdiction. You are thus required to disclose your community property interest in the income received by your spouse from Lockheed.
